name: Checkbox
summary: Accessible boolean checkbox for a single on/off choice, with an optional `label` + `helperText` and an `indeterminate` (mixed) state; controlled via `checked` + `onValueChange`, or auto-bound to a `FormControl` with `name` (keep `bindForm` to opt a form-bound box out of writing the store). A real hidden native input backs it for forms/AT.

props.name: name
props.description:
Field name used for FormControl binding and form submission. When `bindForm` is false,
`name` may be omitted and no form binding/submission occurs.

props.name: bindForm
props.description: Disable FormControl binding and omit name from submission.

props.name: size
props.description: Visual size; token or CSS length.

props.name: onValueChange
props.description: Canonical value change event (fires on each mutation).

props.name: onValueCommit
props.description: Commit event (fires at the same moment for toggles).

props.name: indeterminate
props.description: Mixed (indeterminate) visual state for parent selections.

props.name: label
props.description: Visual label rendered by the component or its wrapper. Accepts string or node.

props.name: helperText
props.description: Supplemental hint or validation content typically rendered under the control.

props.name: error
props.description: Marks the field invalid; components may adjust visuals and helper text color.

props.name: fullWidth
props.description: Stretches the field wrapper to occupy the full available width.

props.name: preset
props.description: One or many style-preset names registered via `definePreset()`

bestPractices: Use Checkbox for independent booleans. For mutually exclusive choices, prefer `RadioGroup`.

Give it an accessible name via the `label` prop (rendered visibly and associated to the native input); the whole row is clickable. With no `label`/children/`aria-label` it dev-warns. Use `helperText` for supplementary guidance — it renders OUTSIDE the label (so it never leaks into the accessible name) and is wired via `aria-describedby`.

Choose control model deliberately. Use `checked` / `onValueChange` when the value participates in app state; `defaultChecked` is fine for local forms; in a form, set a stable `name` so values submit predictably.
Use `indeterminate` for a parent that summarizes a mixed set of children; it shows a dash and exposes the native mixed state to assistive tech.
Pick `size` for the VISUAL box — the touch hit area is already ≥44px on coarse pointers automatically (relaxed under `compact`), so you don't need to size up for tappability.

Colours follow the theme intent contract (the same one Button/IconButton use), so checked fill, the checkmark, the neutral border, and the focus ring stay consistent in light and dark — avoid ad-hoc colour overrides.
Disabled with context. If a checkbox is disabled, explain why nearby; avoid silently disabling without guidance.

examples.title: Controlled checkbox
Label + helperText
examples.code: <Checkbox label='Email me updates' helperText='No more than once a week.' defaultChecked />

examples.title: FormControl binding
